title: Efftee Studios

Initially **Efftee Films** was based in [Melbourne](/wiki/Melbourne "Melbourne") and used optical sound equipment imported from the US.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Australian film and theatre production studio

**Efftee Studios** was an early Australian film and theatre production studio, established by [F.W. Thring](/wiki/F.W._Thring "F.W. Thring") (the name 'Efftee' deriving from his initials, 'FT' for Francis Thring) in 1930. It existed until Thring's death in 1935. Initially **Efftee Films** was based in [Melbourne](/wiki/Melbourne "Melbourne") and used optical sound equipment imported from the US.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Efftee_Studios&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

In 1931, the company produced the first commercially viable Australian made sound feature film, *[Diggers](/wiki/Diggers_(1931_film) "Diggers (1931 film)")*.[[1]](#cite_note-dig-1) Over the next five years, Efftee produced nine features, over 80 shorts and several stage productions, including the Australian musicals *[Collits' Inn](/wiki/Collits%27_Inn_(musical) "Collits' Inn (musical)")* (1933) and *[The Cedar Tree](/wiki/The_Cedar_Tree_(musical) "The Cedar Tree (musical)")* (1934). Notable collaborators include [C. J. Dennis](/wiki/C._J._Dennis "C. J. Dennis"), [George Wallace](/wiki/George_Wallace_(Australian_comedian) "George Wallace (Australian comedian)") and [Frank Harvey](/wiki/Frank_Harvey_(Australian_screenwriter) "Frank Harvey (Australian screenwriter)").

In 1934, Thring suspended Efftee's operations[[2]](#cite_note-2) to pressure the government to establish a quota for Australian films, threatening to move production to London. He relocated production to Sydney to take advantage of the New South Wales Cinematograph Films (Australian Quota) Act 1935.[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)

Efftee was also the first operator of [Melbourne](/wiki/Melbourne,_Australia "Melbourne, Australia") radio station [3XY](/wiki/3XY "3XY") which began broadcasting on 9 September 1935.

Thring traveled to Hollywood in March 1936 to look for scriptwriters and actors[[5]](#cite_note-5) and returned in June but died soon after.

Founder F.W. Thring was the father of the Australian and international actor, [Frank Thring](/wiki/Frank_Thring "Frank Thring").

## Selected filmography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Efftee_Studios&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Selected filmography")]

### Features

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Efftee_Studios&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Features")]

* *[A Co-respondent's Course](/wiki/A_Co-respondent%27s_Course "A Co-respondent's Course")* (1931)[[6]](#cite_note-co-6)
* *[Diggers](/wiki/Diggers_(1931_film) "Diggers (1931 film)")* (1931)
* *[The Haunted Barn](/wiki/The_Haunted_Barn "The Haunted Barn")* (1931)[[7]](#cite_note-barn-7)
* *[The Sentimental Bloke](/wiki/The_Sentimental_Bloke_(1932_film) "The Sentimental Bloke (1932 film)")* (1932)
* *[His Royal Highness](/wiki/His_Royal_Highness_(1932_film) "His Royal Highness (1932 film)")* (1932)[[8]](#cite_note-his-8)
* *[Harmony Row](/wiki/Harmony_Row "Harmony Row")* (1933)[[9]](#cite_note-harmony-9)
* *[A Ticket in Tatts](/wiki/A_Ticket_in_Tatts_(1934_film) "A Ticket in Tatts (1934 film)")* (1934)[[10]](#cite_note-tatts-10)
* *[Sheepmates](/wiki/Sheepmates "Sheepmates")* (1934) – abandoned during filming
* *[The Streets of London](/wiki/The_Streets_of_London_(1934_film) "The Streets of London (1934 film)")* (1934)
* *[Clara Gibbings](/wiki/Clara_Gibbings "Clara Gibbings")* (1934)[[11]](#cite_note-clara-11)

### Non-Efftee features shot in the Efftee Studio

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Efftee_Studios&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Non-Efftee features shot in the Efftee Studio")]

* *[Diggers in Blighty](/wiki/Diggers_in_Blighty "Diggers in Blighty")* (1933)
* *[Waltzing Matilda](/wiki/Waltzing_Matilda_(1933_film) "Waltzing Matilda (1933 film)")* (1933)
* *[Heritage](/wiki/Heritage_(1935_film) "Heritage (1935 film)")* (1935)

### The 'Efftee Entertainers' Variety Shorts

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Efftee_Studios&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: The 'Efftee Entertainers' Variety Shorts")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/69/Minnie_Love.jpg/250px-Minnie_Love.jpg)](/wiki/File:Minnie_Love.jpg)

Actress [Minnie Love](/wiki/Minnie_Love "Minnie Love"), circa 1914

* *Will Cade and his Regent Theatre Orchestra in Selections from '[The Desert Song](/wiki/The_Desert_Song "The Desert Song")'* (1931)
* *Jack O'Hagan – Vocalist Composer* (1931) – with [Jack O'Hagan](/wiki/Jack_O%27Hagan "Jack O'Hagan")
* *Cecil Parkes' Strad Trio in Selections from Their Repertoire* (1931)
* *Athol Tier as Napoleon* (1931)
* *Keith Desmond in Recitations (No. 1)* (1931)
* *Keith Desmond in Recitations (No. 2)* (1931)
* *George Wallace, Australia's Premier Comedian* (1931) – with [George Wallace](/wiki/George_Wallace_(Australian_comedian) "George Wallace (Australian comedian)")
* *Melody and Terpsichor* (1931)
* *Stan Ray and George Moon Jnr, Speciality Dancers (No. 1)* (1931)
* *Stan Ray and George Moon Jnr, Speciality Dancers (No. 2)* (1931)
* *Melbourne's Chinese Orchestra in Selections* (1931)
* *Minnie Love in Impressions of Famous Artists (No. 1)* (1931)
* *Minnie Love in Impressions of Famous Artists (No. 2)* (1931)
* *Minnie Love in Impressions of Famous Artists (No. 3)* (1931)
* *The Sundowners – Harmony Quartette (No. 1)* (1932)
* *The Sundowners – Harmony Quartette (No. 2)* (1932)
* *Lou Vernon – Character Songs (No. 1)* (1932)
* *Kathleen Goodall – Songs at the Piano (No. 1)* (1932)
* *Kathleen Goodall – Songs at the Piano (No. 2)* (1932)
* *Kathleen Goodall – Songs at the Piano (No. 3)* (1932)
* *Peter Bornstein, Celebrated Violinist* (1932)
* *George White* (1932)
* *Miss Ada Reeve – Comedienne (No. 1)* (1932) – with [Ada Reeve](/wiki/Ada_Reeve "Ada Reeve")
* *Miss Ada Reeve – Comedienne (No. 2)* (1932)
* *Miss Byrl Walkley, Soprano* (1932)
* *Somewhere South of Shanghai, Rendered by Marshall Crosby* (1932)
* *Neil McKay, Scottish Comedian* (1932) – with Neil McKay
* *Williamson Imperial Grand Opera Co. Orchestra – Overture from Carmen, by Bizet* (1932)
* *Williamson Imperial Grand Opera Co. Orchestra – Overture from Gounod's Faust* (1932)
* *Williamson Imperial Grand Opera Co. Orchestra – Selections from the Barber of Seville by Rossini* (1932)
* *Signor Apollo Granforte and the Williamson Imperial Grand opera Company Orchestra* (1932)
* *Lou Vernon – Character Songs (No. 2)* (1932)

### Two-Reel Efftee shorts

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Efftee_Studios&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Two-Reel Efftee shorts")]

* *Oh, What a Night!* (1932) – 14-minute short starring [George Wallace](/wiki/George_Wallace_(Australian_comedian) "George Wallace (Australian comedian)")
* *In the Future* (1933) – 12-minute short

## Theatre shows

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Efftee_Studios&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Theatre shows")]

* *[Collit's Inn](/wiki/Collits%27_Inn_(musical) "Collits' Inn (musical)")* (1933)[[12]](#cite_note-12)
* *[The Beloved Vagabond](/w/index.php?title=The_Beloved_Vagabond_(play)&action=edit&redlink=1 "The Beloved Vagabond (play) (page does not exist)")* (1934) – done in Melbourne[[13]](#cite_note-13)
* *[Mother of Pearl](/wiki/Mother_of_Pearl_(play) "Mother of Pearl (play)")* (1934) – done in Melbourne[[14]](#cite_note-14)
* *[Her Past](/w/index.php?title=Her_Past&action=edit&redlink=1 "Her Past (page does not exist)")* (1934)
* *[Jolly Rogers](/w/index.php?title=Jolly_Roger_(play)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Jolly Roger (play) (page does not exist)")* (1934)
* *[Children in Uniform](/wiki/Children_in_Uniform "Children in Uniform")* (1934)[[15]](#cite_note-15)
* *[Clara Gibbings](/wiki/Clara_Gibbings "Clara Gibbings")* (1933) – later filmed
* *[Rope](/wiki/Rope_(play) "Rope (play)")* (1934)[[16]](#cite_note-16)
* *[The Streets of London](/wiki/The_Streets_of_London_(1934_film) "The Streets of London (1934 film)")* (1933)[[17]](#cite_note-17) – later filmed
* *[S.S. Sunshine](/wiki/S.S._Sunshine "S.S. Sunshine")* (1935)
* *[The Cedar Tree](/wiki/The_Cedar_Tree_(musical) "The Cedar Tree (musical)")* (1934)
* *[Crazy Nights Revue](/wiki/Crazy_Nights_Revue "Crazy Nights Revue")* (1935)
* *[The Oojah Bird](/wiki/The_Oojah_Bird "The Oojah Bird")* (1935)

## Unmade films

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Efftee_Studios&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Unmade films")]

* adaptation of *Ginger Murdoch*, a book by [William Hatfield](/wiki/William_Hatfield_(writer) "William Hatfield (writer)"), but it never eventuated[[18]](#cite_note-18)
* adaptation of *Collitt's Inn*
* adaptation of the book *[James! Don't Be a Fool](/wiki/James!_Don%27t_Be_a_Fool "James! Don't Be a Fool")* by [E. V. Timms](/wiki/E._V._Timms "E. V. Timms") with an adaptation by [John P. McLeod](/wiki/John_P._McLeod "John P. McLeod")[[19]](#cite_note-prize-19)
* adaptation of John P. McLeod's book *Along the Road* with a script to be done by McLeod[[19]](#cite_note-prize-19)
* *A Sweepin' the Deep* with George Wallace
* adaptation of *Redheap* by Norman Lindsay – £1,000 reportedly paid for the rights[[20]](#cite_note-everyone-20)
* *[Typhoon Treasure](/wiki/Typhoon_Treasure "Typhoon Treasure")* – later made by others
* adaptation of *Robbery Under Arms* as a stage musical then a film

## See also

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Efftee_Studios&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: See also")]

* [Cinema of Australia](/wiki/Cinema_of_Australia "Cinema of Australia")
